title: Bernhardia tannensis

uses: Bernhardia tannensis has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in West Africa, where it is valued for its antimalarial and anti-inflammatory properties. Its roots and leaves are used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant is also used to treat respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Furthermore, it is believed to have antimicrobial properties, which can help to prevent the spread of infections. Traditional healers in West Africa continue to use Bernhardia tannensis to treat a range of health conditions.
